name: Iglesia de San Jorge and Palacio de los Hormillos in Las Fraguas to Ermita del Moral
name_original: Iglesia de San Jorge y Palacio de los Hormillos en Las Fraguas a la Ermita del Moral
region_intro: Touring cycling around Arenas De Iguña, located in Cantabria, Spain, offers a diverse landscape for cyclists. The region features the scenic Besaya River winding through rocky formations and includes parts of the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, known for its mountain prairies and rich biodiversity. Terrain varies from predominantly flat, family-friendly routes along expanded cycling networks to more challenging ascents towards areas like Monte Obios and the El Moral Plateau. Historical paths, including sections of the old Besaya road, also contribute to the varied cycling experience. Best touring cycling routes around Arenas De Iguña The most popular touring cycling route is Iglesia de San Jorge and Palacio de los Hormillos in Las Fraguas to Ermita del Moral, a 25.7 miles (41.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 39 minutes to complete. This route connects historical landmarks with high-mountain landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Iglesia de Santa María, Potes – Main Street of Riocorvo loop from Lombera, a difficult 26.4 miles (42.6 km) path. This route incorporates historical sites and local village scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Los Corrales de Buelna – Palacio de los Hornillos loop from Arenas de Iguña, a 37.9 miles (61.1 km) trail leading through areas near the notable Palacio de los Hornillos, often completed in about 4 hours 42 minutes. Touring cycling around Arenas De Iguña is defined by river valleys, historical routes, and varied terrain from flat paths to forest tracks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from family-friendly rides to more challenging ascents. The routes in Arenas De Iguña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Arenas De Iguña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Arenas De Iguña?
A: There are over 45 touring cycling routes available in Arenas De Iguña, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has recorded 47 routes in total.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Arenas De Iguña?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from predominantly flat, family-friendly routes along expanded cycling networks to more challenging ascents towards areas like Monte Obios and the El Moral Plateau. You'll find valley roads, historical paths, and forest tracks. Some routes may require gravel tires of at least 40mm for optimal comfort.

Q: Are there options for less experienced touring cyclists in Arenas De Iguña?
A: Yes, Arenas De Iguña has routes suitable for beginners and families. The local town hall has expanded a network of cycling itineraries, including longitudinal and transversal routes, which are described as 'family-friendly' due to their predominantly flat terrain. There are 3 easy and 9 moderate routes available, making it accessible for varying skill levels.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring in Arenas De Iguña?
A: The touring cycling routes in Arenas De Iguña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the region's varied terrain, often praising the scenic beauty and diverse options.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Arenas De Iguña?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24635280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Corrales de Buelna – Palacio de los Hornillos loop from Arenas de Iguña&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 61.1 km trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31870145" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de Santa María, Potes – Main Street of Riocorvo loop from Lombera&lt;/a&gt;, which incorporates historical sites and local village scenery.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by historical landmarks or cultural sites?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can explore routes that follow sections of the old Besaya road, once a Roman road and pilgrimage route to Santiago. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012458519/iglesia-de-san-jorge-and-palacio-de-los-hormillos-in-las-fraguas-to-ermita-del-moral" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de San Jorge and Palacio de los Hormillos in Las Fraguas to Ermita del Moral&lt;/a&gt; route connects historical landmarks with high-mountain landscapes. The grand &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4243290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio de los Hornillos&lt;/a&gt; in Las Fraguas is a notable architectural highlight.

Q: Can I find routes with viewpoints or scenic natural features?
A: Yes, Arenas De Iguña offers stunning natural vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4566932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau&lt;/a&gt;, reachable via a forest track, provides amazing high-mountain landscapes. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, an accessible natural attraction perfect for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4834775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Nuestra Señora de los Remedios&lt;/a&gt; offers unparalleled panoramic views of the Cantabrian landscape.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for advanced touring cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: Yes, for advanced cyclists, there are 35 difficult routes available. Some routes, like the 'Ruta con subida al "Pombo"' mentioned in regional research, offer more challenging ascents. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Brazo Refuge – Palacio de los Hornillos loop from Arenas de Iguña&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 45.9 km route with significant elevation gain, perfect for those seeking a demanding ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Arenas De Iguña?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, the region's diverse terrain and natural beauty suggest that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for touring cycling. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially on routes with shade or higher elevations.

Q: Are there any bike rental services available in the area?
A: Yes, for those without their own equipment, La Posada Ochohermanas in Las Fraguas provides electric bike rentals, including touring bikes, mountain bikes, and 'bigfoot' bikes. They also offer accessories like baby seats and trail gators, catering to various needs.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in Arenas De Iguña?
A: Yes, several routes offer significant distances. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24635280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Corrales de Buelna – Palacio de los Hornillos loop from Arenas de Iguña&lt;/a&gt; covers 61.1 km, providing a substantial ride through the area. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012458519/iglesia-de-san-jorge-and-palacio-de-los-hormillos-in-las-fraguas-to-ermita-del-moral" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de San Jorge and Palacio de los Hormillos in Las Fraguas to Ermita del Moral&lt;/a&gt;, which is 41.3 km long.

Q: What natural parks or reserves can I explore by bike in Arenas De Iguña?
A: Arenas De Iguña is located within or in close proximity to the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, particularly near the headwaters of the Llares River. This expansive natural park is renowned for its impressive landscapes and rich biodiversity, including mountain prairies known as 'brañas'. You can find routes that traverse or offer views into this beautiful protected area.
tips: El Moral is a high mountain plateau which is reached by a forest track that is truly amazing. Some amazing landscapes and very close to here is the hermitage that is usually used as a refuge and a place to take a break.

Mythical hermitage with a sufficiently equipped refuge. It is located on the top of a hill halfway between Barcena Mayor and Arenas de Iguña. The site is very quiet with fresh air and cows resting in the green meadows. A place outside of time and the world. It is part of an obligatory step in the demanding cycling test of the 10,000 Soplao.

The church of San Jorge, popularly known as El Partenón, is a few meters from the Palacio de los Hornillos. Built in 1890 as a chapel near his palace, it was donated by the dukes to the town of Las Fraguas to serve as a parish church. It was used during the Civil War as a prison. It is a purely neoclassical building.

The Palacio de los Hornillos or Palacio de las Fraguas is a large estate in which two palaces are located. The oldest called Casona de las Fraguas (from the 18th century) and the largest of both, called de los Hornillos (late 19th century). It has been the setting for several movies. The Palace of the Hornillos is a private property, owned by the Duke of San Carlos, and cannot be visited. La Casona de las Fraguas is currently a catering business that organizes banquets and celebrations.

waypoints.name: Church of San Jorge (The Parthenon of Las Fraguas)
